title: Epitaph For An English Soldier And An Indian Soldier Buried Together In France
content:
When the fierce bugle thrilled alarm,
        From lands apart these fighters came.
    An equal courage nerved each arm,
        And stirred each generous heart to flame.

    Now, greatly dead, they lie below;
        Their creed or language no man heeds,
    Since for their colour they can show
        The blood-red blazon of their deeds!
meaning:
"Epitaph For An English Soldier And An Indian Soldier Buried Together In France" is a quintessential example of R. C. Lehmann's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
